html:
[1265]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大历中 - A time period during the Tang Dynasty, specifically the Dali era (766-779).
  • 有才人 - Refers to a talented woman, often used in the context of court or palace.
  • 丐食 - Means 'to beg for food,' indicating a life of poverty.
  • 衢路 - Refers to a busy street or thoroughfare.
  • 察之 - Means 'to observe' or 'to notice.'
  • 媚色 - Refers to a charming or attractive appearance.
  • 纳为姬 - Means 'to take as a concubine.'
  • 舍其父于后户 - Means 'to place her father in the back of the house.'
  • 优给之 - Means 'to provide for him generously.'
  • 颖悟绝伦 - Means 'exceptionally intelligent and perceptive.'
  • 自撰歌 - Means 'to compose her own songs.'
  • 古《长命西河女》 - Refers to an ancient song, possibly a reference to a specific piece of music.
  • 加减其节奏 - Means 'to modify its rhythm.'
  • 颇有新声 - Means 'has a new and unique sound.'
  • 未进闻 - Means 'not yet heard by the emperor.'
  • 先侑歌于青 - Means 'first performed the song for General Wei Qing.'
  • 召红红于屏风后听之 - Means 'called Zhang Hong Hong to listen behind the screen.'
  • 以小豆数合记其拍 - Means 'used small beans to keep track of the beats.'
  • 隔屏风歌之 - Means 'sang the song behind the screen.'
  • 一声不失 - Means 'not missing a single note.'
  • 寻达上听 - Means 'soon reached the ears of the emperor.'
  • 召入宜春院 - Means 'was summoned into the Yichun Palace.'
  • 宠泽隆异 - Means 'received extraordinary favor.'
  • 宫中号“曲娘子” - Means 'was known in the palace as 'Song Girl.''
  • 寻为才人 - Means 'soon became a court lady.'
  • 内史奏韦青卒 - Means 'the Grand Historian reported that General Wei Qing had died.'
  • 妾本风尘丐者 - Means 'I was originally a beggar from the streets.'
  • 致身入内 - Means 'to enter the palace.'
  • 皆自韦青 - Means 'all thanks to General Wei Qing.'
  • 不忍忘其恩 - Means 'cannot forget his kindness.'
  • 一恸而绝 - Means 'died of grief.'
  • 嘉叹之 - Means 'praised and lamented her.'
  • 即赠昭仪 - Means 'posthumously granted the title of Zhaoyi (a high-ranking imperial concubine).'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大历中 - The Dali era (766-779) of the Tang Dynasty, a period of significant cultural and artistic development.
  • 才人 - A rank in the imperial harem, indicating a high level of talent and favor.
  • 韦青 - A general during the Tang Dynasty, known for his military prowess and patronage of the arts.
  • 宜春院 - A palace or residence within the imperial complex, often associated with imperial concubines.
  • 曲娘子 - A nickname given to Zhang Hong Hong, reflecting her talent in music and singing.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking title in the imperial harem, equivalent to a senior concubine.

Proper Nouns

  • 张红红 - The name of the talented woman.
  • 韦青 - The name of the general.
  • 宜春院 - The name of the palace or residence.
  • 曲娘子 - The nickname given to Zhang Hong Hong.
  • 昭仪 - The posthumous title granted to Zhang Hong Hong.

[1266]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不免行丐 - Means 'could not help but beg,' indicating a state of poverty and dependence.
  • 遂达至尊 - Means 'thus reached the highest position,' suggesting a dramatic rise in status.
  • 人有绝技，定不埋没 - Means 'a person with extraordinary skills will not be forgotten,' emphasizing the idea that talent will eventually be recognized.
  • 知音之难遇 - Means 'it is difficult to find a true appreciator,' highlighting the rarity of finding someone who truly understands one's value.
  • 蒙识拔 - Means 'was recognized and promoted,' indicating a significant change in one's circumstances.
  • 卒以死报 - Means 'finally repaid with death,' suggesting a deep sense of gratitude and loyalty.
  • 伯牙氏之琴 - Refers to the famous story of Bo Ya and his zither, symbolizing a deep and rare friendship or bond.
  • 见岭下遗骸，伤之 - Means 'saw the remains of the dead on the mountain and was moved by them,' expressing a strong emotional response.
  • 具畚锸 - Means 'prepared shovels and spades,' indicating preparation for burial.
  • 自为瘗埋 - Means 'buried them himself,' showing personal involvement in the act of burial.
  • 妾王玉英也 - Means 'I am Wang Yu Ying,' introducing the character.
  • 家世湘潭 - Means 'my family is from Xiangtan,' providing background information.
  • 父为闽守，将兵御胡元，战死 - Means 'my father was the governor of Fujian, led troops against the Yuan dynasty, and died in battle,' describing the father's heroic but tragic fate.
  • 妾不肯辱，与其家死岭下 - Means 'I would not be dishonored, so I died with my family on the mountain,' indicating a strong sense of honor and loyalty.
  • 岁久，骸骨偶出 - Means 'after many years, the bones were exposed,' describing the discovery of the remains.
  • 蒙公覆掩，恩最深重 - Means 'you covered and buried us, your kindness is very profound,' expressing deep gratitude.
  • 虽不可谓非人，理有冥合 - Means 'though I am not human, there is a mysterious connection,' suggesting a supernatural or spiritual bond.
  • 君其勿疑 - Means 'please do not doubt,' reassuring the listener.
  • 遂与合 - Means 'and we became intimate,' indicating a romantic or sexual relationship.
  • 亡何，子生 - Means 'soon after, a son was born,' marking a significant event.
  • 孕以七月七日 - Means 'conceived on the seventh day of the seventh month,' indicating the date of conception.
  • 庆云母亦微知其事 - Means 'Qingyun's mother also vaguely knew about the matter,' suggesting partial awareness.
  • 急欲见孙 - Means 'hurriedly wanted to see the grandson,' expressing a strong desire.
  • 抱归 - Means 'took him back home,' describing the action.
  • 儿受阳气尚浅，未可令人遽见 - Means 'the child has not yet fully absorbed the yang energy, so he should not be shown to others yet,' indicating a belief in the child's vulnerability.
  • 忽母来登楼 - Means 'suddenly the mother came up to the house,' describing an unexpected event.
  • 女已抱子从窗牖逸去 - Means 'the woman had already taken the child and fled through the window,' indicating a sudden departure.
  • 察之乃蜂房也 - Means 'upon closer inspection, it was a beehive,' revealing a surprising discovery.
  • 抱儿归湘潭 - Means 'took the child back to Xiangtan,' describing the final action.
  • 无主者，乃故弃之河旁 - Means 'there was no one to claim him, so he was abandoned by the river,' indicating the child's abandonment.
  • 书衣带间曰：“十八年后当来归。” - Means 'wrote on the child's clothes, 'After eighteen years, you will return.'', providing a prophetic message.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红红 - A name that may have symbolic or cultural significance, possibly referring to a specific individual or a character in a story.
  • 韦青 - A name that may have symbolic or cultural significance, possibly referring to a specific individual or a character in a story.
  • 伯牙氏之琴 - Refers to the famous story of Bo Ya and his zither, symbolizing a deep and rare friendship or bond.
  • 湘潭 - A place name, indicating a specific region in China.
  • 闽守 - Refers to the governor of Fujian, a significant political position.
  • 胡元 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty, a period of Mongol rule in China.
  • 七月七日 - Refers to the Qixi Festival, a traditional Chinese festival celebrating the meeting of the Cowherd and Weaver Girl.
  • 蜂房 - Refers to a beehive, which may have symbolic or cultural significance.

Proper Nouns

  • 红红 - A name that may have symbolic or cultural significance.
  • 韦青 - A name that may have symbolic or cultural significance.
  • 湘潭 - A place name, indicating a specific region in China.
  • 闽守 - Refers to the governor of Fujian.
  • 胡元 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty.
  • 七月七日 - Refers to the Qixi Festival.
  • 蜂房 - Refers to a beehive.

[1267]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 富而无子 - Wealthy but childless, indicating a man's desire for an heir.
  • 拾之 - Colloquial expression meaning 'to adopt' or 'to take in.'
  • 清癯敏慧异常儿 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'slender, intelligent, and exceptionally wise child.'
  • 名曰鹤龄 - Formal expression meaning 'named as鹤龄.'
  • 旋生二子 - Phrase meaning 'soon after, two more sons were born.'
  • 授室 - Formal expression meaning 'to marry off.'
  • 泥衣带中语 - Phrase meaning 'words in the mud and belt,' referring to a mysterious or cryptic message.
  • 未决 - Phrase meaning 'not yet decided,' indicating uncertainty.
  • 捐金四十 - Phrase meaning 'donated forty units of gold.'
  • 委禽于其里易氏 - Phrase meaning 'entrusted a fowl to the village's易氏,' indicating a formal engagement.
  • 尝以二竹筴与生 - Phrase meaning 'once gave two bamboo sticks to the boy,' indicating a gift.
  • 击筴则女即至 - Phrase meaning 'when the boy hits the sticks, the girl will come,' indicating a magical or ritualistic action.
  • 有疾痛祸患 - Phrase meaning 'if there is illness, pain, or disaster.'
  • 得女一语 - Phrase meaning 'if the girl speaks a word,' indicating her power to provide relief.
  • 疑女为妖 - Phrase meaning 'suspected the girl of being a demon.'
  • 诬生失行 - Phrase meaning 'accused the boy of immoral behavior.'
  • 淫主人女 - Phrase meaning 'committing adultery with the master's daughter.'
  • 褫去章服 - Phrase meaning 'stripped of his official robes,' indicating a loss of status or honor.
  • 渐疏 - Phrase meaning 'gradually becoming distant.'
  • 相期惟一岁一来 - Phrase meaning 'agreed to meet only once a year.'
  • 来必以七月七夕 - Phrase meaning 'to come on the seventh day of the seventh month.'
  • 儿生已符衣带之期 - Phrase meaning 'the child's birth has fulfilled the prophecy in the belt.'
  • 伪作星家言谒黄公 - Phrase meaning 'pretended to be an astrologer to visit Mr. Huang.'
  • 傥不寒盟 - Phrase meaning 'if you do not break the oath.'
  • 有衣带语在 - Phrase meaning 'there are words in the belt.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湘潭 - Place name, referring to a city in Hunan Province, China.
  • 黄公 - Proper noun, referring to a wealthy man named Huang.
  • 鹤龄 - Proper noun, referring to the adopted child's name.
  • 鹤算、二龄 - Proper nouns, referring to the names of Huang's two biological sons.
  • 制举之业 - Refers to the imperial examination system, indicating the education and career path of the sons.
  • 衣带语 - Refers to a cryptic message left in the belt, indicating a mysterious or prophetic element.
  • 委禽 - Traditional Chinese custom of proposing marriage by presenting a fowl.
  • 七月七夕 - Refers to the Qixi Festival, a traditional Chinese festival celebrated on the seventh day of the seventh month of the lunar calendar.
  • 褫去章服 - Refers to the act of stripping someone of their official robes, indicating a loss of status or honor.

Proper Nouns

  • 湘潭 - Place name, referring to a city in Hunan Province, China.
  • 黄公 - Proper noun, referring to a wealthy man named Huang.
  • 鹤龄 - Proper noun, referring to the adopted child's name.
  • 鹤算、二龄 - Proper nouns, referring to the names of Huang's two biological sons.
  • 易氏 - Proper noun, referring to the family to whom the engagement was made.

[1268]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 固也 - Colloquial expression meaning 'indeed' or 'certainly,' used to confirm a statement.
  • 阿保 - Term of endearment meaning 'nurturer' or 'caretaker,' often used for a mother or close relative.
  • 委禽之资 - Metaphor for a dowry or marriage funds, literally meaning 'funds for the exchange of birds' (a traditional marriage ritual).
  • 不胜情 - Means 'overwhelmed with emotion,' indicating deep feelings.
  • 浮湘 - Reference to drifting on the Xiang River, symbolizing a journey or wandering.
  • 阴为卫 - Means 'secretly protect' or 'guard covertly,' implying discreet assistance.
  • 经纪其资斧 - Means 'manage or arrange their funds,' where '资斧' refers to financial resources.
  • 恚恨 - Means 'resentment' or 'anger,' indicating strong negative emotions.
  • 待耳 - Colloquial expression meaning 'waiting' or 'awaiting,' often used to imply expectation.
  • 狐媚 - Derogatory term meaning 'fox charm' or 'seductive allure,' often used to describe deceitful or manipulative behavior.
  • 贞妇 - Refers to a virtuous or chaste woman.
  • 行露 - Refers to a poem from the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), often interpreted as a metaphor for moral integrity.
  • 凯风 - Refers to a poem from the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), often interpreted as a metaphor for maternal love or nurturing.
  • 洞里仙人路不遥 - Poetic expression meaning 'the path of the immortals in the cave is not far,' symbolizing a spiritual or transcendent journey.
  • 洞庭烟雨昼潇潇 - Poetic description of the misty rain over Dongting Lake, evoking a sense of melancholy or longing.
  • 乌鹊桥 - Refers to the Bridge of Magpies, a mythological bridge in Chinese folklore, often associated with love and reunion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 公曰 - Formal way of introducing a statement, often used in classical texts to indicate a speaker.
  • 沟壑 - Metaphor for a grave or a place of death, often used to describe a tragic end.
  • 还珠 - Metaphor for returning something valuable, often used to describe a gesture of goodwill or repayment.
  • 应试长沙去也 - Refers to a journey to Changsha for an examination, indicating a pursuit of academic or official success.
  • 浮湘 - Reference to drifting on the Xiang River, symbolizing a journey or wandering.
  • 洞庭烟雨 - Poetic description of the misty rain over Dongting Lake, evoking a sense of melancholy or longing.
  • 乌鹊桥 - Refers to the Bridge of Magpies, a mythological bridge in Chinese folklore, often associated with love and reunion.

Proper Nouns

  • 公 - Respectful title for a man, often used in classical texts.
  • 沟壑 - Metaphor for a grave or a place of death, often used to describe a tragic end.
  • 长沙 - Place name Changsha.
  • 闽 - Place name Fujian.
  • 狐媚 - Derogatory term meaning 'fox charm' or 'seductive allure,' often used to describe deceitful or manipulative behavior.
  • 行露 - Poem from the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*).
  • 凯风 - Poem from the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*).
  • 洞里仙人路不遥 - Poetic expression meaning 'the path of the immortals in the cave is not far.'
  • 洞庭烟雨 - Poetic description of the misty rain over Dongting Lake.
  • 乌鹊桥 - Mythological bridge in Chinese folklore, often associated with love and reunion.

[1269]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 莫讶鸳鸯会有缘 - Poetic expression meaning 'Do not be surprised that the mandarin ducks have a destined connection.' '鸳鸯' (mandarin ducks) symbolize love and fidelity in Chinese culture.
  • 桃花结子已千年 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'The peach blossoms have borne fruit for a thousand years,' symbolizing long-lasting love or a relationship that has endured for a long time.
  • 尘心不释蓝桥路 - Poetic expression meaning 'The worldly heart does not let go of the path to Lan Bridge.' '蓝桥' (Lan Bridge) is a reference to a legendary bridge where lovers were separated.
  • 信是蓬莱有谪仙 - Poetic expression meaning 'Indeed, there is a demoted immortal in the fairyland of Penglai.' '谪仙' (demoted immortal) refers to a legendary figure who was exiled from heaven.
  • 朝暮云骖闽楚关 - Poetic expression meaning 'Morning and evening, the clouds ride the carriages between the passes of Min and Chu.' '云骖' (cloud carriages) refers to mythical vehicles.
  • 青鸾信不继尘寰 - Poetic expression meaning 'The blue phoenix does not follow the mortal world.' '青鸾' (blue phoenix) is a mythical bird symbolizing messages or good fortune.
  • 乍逢仙侣抛桃灯 - Poetic expression meaning 'Suddenly meeting a fairy companion, throwing the peach lamp.' '桃灯' (peach lamp) is a mythical object.
  • 笑我沮波照雾鬟 - Poetic expression meaning 'Laughing at me, the muddy waves reflect the misty hair.' '雾鬟' (misty hair) refers to the hair of a fairy or a beautiful woman.
  • 万鸟鸣春 - Poetic expression meaning 'Ten thousand birds sing in spring,' symbolizing the beauty and vitality of spring.
  • 委禽四十金之费 - Poetic expression meaning 'The cost of forty gold coins for the dowry.' '委禽' (dowry) refers to the traditional gift given by the groom's family to the bride's family.
  • 瘗骨掩骼 - Poetic expression meaning 'Burying bones and covering skulls,' symbolizing the act of burying the dead.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鸳鸯 - Mandarin ducks, a symbol of love and fidelity in Chinese culture.
  • 桃花 - Peach blossoms, symbolizing love and long-lasting relationships.
  • 蓝桥 - A legendary bridge where lovers were separated, symbolizing separation and longing.
  • 蓬莱 - A mythical fairyland in Chinese mythology.
  • 谪仙 - A legendary figure who was exiled from heaven, often used to describe a talented or extraordinary person.
  • 闽楚关 - Refers to the passes between the regions of Min and Chu, symbolizing a journey or transition.
  • 青鸾 - A mythical bird symbolizing messages or good fortune.
  • 桃灯 - A mythical object, often associated with fairy tales or legends.
  • 万鸟鸣春 - A poetic expression symbolizing the beauty and vitality of spring.
  • 委禽 - The traditional dowry given by the groom's family to the bride's family.
  • 瘗骨掩骼 - The act of burying the dead, symbolizing compassion and respect for the deceased.

Proper Nouns

  • 周廷章 - The name of the author or compiler of the text.
  • 天顺 - The era name of the Ming dynasty, referring to the reign of Emperor Yingzong.
  • 临安卫 - A military unit in the Ming dynasty.
  • 王指挥 - A military rank, referring to a commander.
  • 广西苗蛮 - Refers to the Miao and Yao ethnic groups in Guangxi.
  • 河南南阳卫 - A military unit in the Ming dynasty.
  • 娇鸾 - The name of the daughter.
  • 娇凤 - The name of the other daughter.
  • 曹姨 - The name of the aunt, who is married to a man named Cao.

[1270]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 值清明节 - Refers to the Qingming Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday for honoring ancestors and visiting graves.
  • 鸾与曹姨率诸婢戏秋千于后园 - Describes the activity of swinging on swings in the garden, a common pastime during the Qingming Festival.
  • 忽闻人声，惊视，则墙缺处有美少年窥视称羡 - Describes the sudden appearance of a handsome young man peering through a gap in the wall and admiring the scene.
  • 鸾大惊走匿，遗罗帕于地 - Describes the reaction of the lady, who is startled and hides, leaving her handkerchief behind.
  • 生逾垣拾去 - Describes the young man climbing over the wall to pick up the handkerchief.
  • 方展玩间，旋有侍女来园寻觅 - Describes the young man unfolding and playing with the handkerchief when a servant girl comes to the garden to search for it.
  • 周折数次，生笑曰：“物入人手，尚何觅耶？” - Describes the young man's playful response to the servant girl's search.
  • 侍女曰：“郎君收得，乞以见还。” - Describes the servant girl's request for the young man to return the handkerchief.
  • 生问：“此帕谁人之物？” - Describes the young man asking who the handkerchief belongs to.
  • 侍儿曰：“鸾姐，主人爱女也。” - Describes the servant girl identifying the handkerchief as belonging to the lady, the master's beloved daughter.
  • 生曰：“若鸾姐自来，当即奉璧。” - Describes the young man's promise to return the handkerchief if the lady comes herself.
  • 侍女叩生姓氏，并家远近 - Describes the servant girl asking the young man for his name and where he is from.
  • 生曰：“周姓，廷章名，苏州吴江人也。父为本学司教，随任于此。与尊府只一墙之隔。久闻尊姐精于文事，仆有小诗，烦为一致。如得报言，帕可还矣。” - Describes the young man introducing himself and his background, and offering to write a poem for the lady in exchange for the handkerchief.
  • 女急于得帕，允之 - Describes the servant girl agreeing to the young man's request.
  • 生逾垣而出，少顷复至，以桃花笺叠成方胜，授女，女返命 - Describes the young man returning the handkerchief with a poem written on a peach blossom paper.
  • 鸾发缄，得一绝云 - Describes the lady opening the poem and reading it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 清明节 - The Qingming Festival, a significant traditional Chinese holiday.
  • 秋千 - Swings, a popular activity during the Qingming Festival.
  • 罗帕 - A handkerchief, often used as a symbol of affection or a token of exchange.
  • 方胜 - A decorative pattern, often used in traditional Chinese art and literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 鸾 - The lady, the master's beloved daughter.
  • 曹姨 - The lady's aunt.
  • 苏州吴江 - The place name, Suzhou, Wujiang.

[1271]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 帕出佳人分外香 - Metaphorical phrase where '帕出佳人' (a beautiful woman emerging from a handkerchief) suggests the woman's beauty is enhanced by her surroundings or by being revealed.
  • 天公教付有情郎 - Poetic expression meaning 'Heaven has entrusted her to a loving man,' implying divine favor or destiny.
  • 殷勤寄取相思句 - Phrase meaning 'to earnestly send lines of longing,' referring to the act of writing love poems.
  • 拟作红丝入洞房 - Metaphorical phrase where '红丝' (red thread) symbolizes the thread of fate, and '洞房' (nuptial chamber) refers to the bridal chamber. The entire phrase suggests the union of lovers under the auspices of fate.
  • 妾身一点玉无瑕 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'my body is as pure as unblemished jade,' emphasizing the woman's purity and virtue.
  • 产自侯门将相家 - Phrase meaning 'born into the family of nobles and generals,' indicating a noble background.
  • 静里有亲同对月 - Phrase meaning 'in quiet moments, she shares the moon with her loved one,' suggesting a peaceful and intimate relationship.
  • 闲中无事独看花 - Phrase meaning 'in idle moments, she alone admires flowers,' implying a solitary and contemplative life.
  • 碧梧只许来奇凤 - Metaphorical phrase where '碧梧' (green phoenix tree) and '奇凤' (wonderful phoenix) symbolize a match between a noble woman and a worthy man.
  • 翠竹那容入老鸦 - Metaphorical phrase where '翠竹' (green bamboo) and '老鸦' (crow) symbolize a mismatch between a noble woman and an unworthy man.
  • 寄语异乡孤零客 - Phrase meaning 'to send a message to a lonely guest in a foreign land,' suggesting a letter to a lover who is far away.
  • 莫将心事乱如麻 - Phrase meaning 'do not let your heart be as tangled as hemp,' advising the recipient not to be overly distressed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 帕出佳人 - A reference to the beauty of a woman, often used in poetry to describe a woman's grace and charm.
  • 天公 - Refers to Heaven, often personified in classical Chinese literature as a benevolent force.
  • 红丝 - A symbol of fate or destiny, often used in Chinese folklore and literature to represent the connection between two people.
  • 洞房 - Refers to the bridal chamber, a traditional setting for the consummation of marriage.
  • 碧梧 - A type of tree, often associated with the phoenix in Chinese mythology, symbolizing nobility and virtue.
  • 翠竹 - Green bamboo, often associated with purity and resilience in Chinese culture.
  • 老鸦 - Crows, often used in Chinese literature to symbolize negative or undesirable qualities.
  • 异乡孤零客 - A reference to a person who is far from home and feeling lonely, often used in poetry to express feelings of homesickness and longing.

Proper Nouns

  • No proper nouns in the passage.

[1272]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 侍兒捧詩至園 - A servant girl brings the poem to the garden. This is a straightforward description but carries the connotation of a messenger or intermediary.
  • 生已候於牆缺矣 - The young man (生) has been waiting at the wall's opening. '牆缺' can be translated as 'wall opening' or 'gap in the wall.'
  • 诗句往返数次 - The poems have been exchanged several times. '诗句' refers to lines of poetry.
  • 侍女得赂，喜于传送 - The servant girl received a bribe and was happy to deliver the messages. '得赂' means 'received a bribe.'
  • 不复言罗帕之事 - She no longer mentioned the handkerchief matter. '罗帕' refers to a silk handkerchief, often used as a token of affection.
  • 适端阳节 - It was the Duanwu Festival. '端阳' is another name for the Dragon Boat Festival.
  • 王治酒园中家宴 - The host (王) prepared a feast in the garden. '治酒' means to prepare or host a banquet.
  • 锦葵空有向阳心 - The hibiscus (锦葵) has a heart that longs for the sun but remains unfulfilled. This is a metaphorical expression.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 二妖相妒，两败俱伤 - Proverbial expression that can be read as 'when two rivals envy each other, both end up losing.'
  • 凡相妒未有不相伤者 - General statement that can be read as 'envy always leads to harm.'
  • 岂独二妖哉 - Rhetorical question that can be read as 'is it only the two demons who are affected?'
  • 申屠澄者，贞元九年，自黄衣调补汉州什邠尉 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'Shentu Cheng was a person, in the ninth year of the Zhenyuan era, he was appointed as the magistrate of Shibi in Han Prefecture from the rank of Huangyi.'
  • 之官，至贞符县东十里许，遇风雪大寒，马不能进 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'going to the post, he arrived at the east of Zhenfu County, about ten li away, where he encountered a severe wind and snowstorm, and the horse could not move forward.'
  • 见路傍有茅舍，中有烟火甚温，乃往就之 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'seeing a thatched cottage by the road, with a warm fire inside, he went to take refuge in it.'
  • 有老父、妪及处女，环火而坐 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'there was an old man, an old woman, and a young girl, sitting around the fire.'
  • 女年方十四五，虽蓬发垢衣，而雪肤花脸，举止妍媚 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'the girl was about fourteen or fifteen years old, although her hair was disheveled and her clothes dirty, she had white skin and a beautiful face, and her demeanor was graceful and charming.'
  • 父、妪见澄来，遽起曰 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'the old man and the old woman saw Shentu Cheng coming and quickly stood up, saying.'
  • 客甚冲寒雪，请前就火 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'you must be very cold from the snow, please come closer to the fire.'
  • 欣谢之 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'he happily thanked them.'
  • 坐良久，天色已暝，风雪不止 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'they sat for a long time, the sky had already darkened, and the wind and snow did not stop.'
  • 西去县尚远，请宿于此可乎 - Nominal sentence that can be read as 'the county is still far to the west, may I stay here for the night?'

  • 玄宗治骊山，起造华清宫 - Means 'Emperor Xuanzong built the Huaqing Palace on Lishan,' a famous palace known for its hot springs.
  • 作朝元阁，立长生殿 - Means 'constructed the Chaoyuan Pavilion and the Changsheng Hall,' indicating the construction of additional buildings within the palace complex.
  • 以馀财因修此寺 - Means 'used the remaining funds to repair this temple,' suggesting that some of the resources were allocated for the maintenance of a nearby temple.

  • 妃子谓帝曰：‘当于飞之秋，不当令东幢岿然无偶。’ - Means 'the concubine said to the emperor, 'It should be appropriate for this season of flying (referring to the spring season), not to leave the eastern pagoda standing alone without a pair.''

  • 吾用圣人之经以临邪孽，如将汤沃残雪耳 - This metaphorical statement explains the effectiveness of the classical text in dispelling the evil.

  • 侑酒 - Literal translation: 'to serve wine.' '侑' means 'to accompany' or 'to serve,' and '酒' means 'wine.' This phrase refers to serving wine during a banquet.
  • 法尧禅舜 - Literal translation: 'to emulate the abdication of Yao to Shun.' '法' means 'to emulate' or 'to follow,' '尧' and '舜' are the names of ancient Chinese sage rulers. This phrase means 'to emulate the abdication of Yao to Shun.'
  • 高皇帝 - Literal translation: 'High Emperor.' Refers to the first emperor of the Han dynasty, Liu Bang, who established the Han dynasty after the fall of the Qin dynasty.
  • 承宗庙 - Literal translation: 'to inherit the ancestral temple.' '承' means 'to inherit' or 'to carry on,' '宗庙' refers to the ancestral temple. This phrase means 'to inherit the ancestral temple.'
  • 传子孙于无穷 - Literal translation: 'to pass on to descendants forever.' '传' means 'to pass on,' '子孙' means 'descendants,' '于无穷' means 'forever.' This phrase means 'to pass on to descendants forever.'
  • 天子无戏言 - Literal translation: 'the emperor has no idle talk.' '天子' means 'emperor,' '无' means 'no,' '戏言' means 'idle talk' or 'jest.' This phrase means 'the emperor has no idle talk.'
  • 闳进曰 - Literal translation: 'Wang Hong stepped forward and said.' '闳' is likely a name, '进曰' means 'stepped forward and said.' This phrase indicates that Wang Hong stepped forward and spoke.
  • 后不得复侍宴 - Literal translation: 'could not attend the banquet again.' '后' means 'later,' '不得' means 'could not,' '复' means 'again,' '侍宴' means 'attend the banquet.' This phrase means 'could not attend the banquet again.'
  • 贤弟新成功坚 - Literal translation: 'Xian's younger brother had a recent achievement.' '贤弟' means 'younger brother of the virtuous one,' '新' means 'new,' '成功坚' is unclear but likely refers to a recent achievement or a name.
  • 外大门无故自坏 - Literal translation: 'the outer gate collapsed without reason.' '外大门' means 'outer gate,' '无故' means 'without reason,' '自坏' means 'collapsed' or 'broke down.' This phrase means 'the outer gate collapsed without reason.'
  • 贤心恶之 - Literal translation: 'Xian was displeased by it.' '贤' is likely a name, '心恶之' means 'was displeased by it.' This phrase indicates that the person was displeased by the collapse of the gate.
  • 后数月，哀帝崩 - Literal translation: 'after several months, Emperor Ai died.' '后数月' means 'after several months,' '哀帝崩' means 'Emperor Ai died.' This phrase indicates that Emperor Ai died after several months.
  • 太皇太后召大司马贤，引见东厢 - Literal translation: 'the Grand Empress Dowager summoned the Grand Marshal Xian and introduced him to the eastern hall.' '太皇太后' means 'Grand Empress Dowager,' '召' means 'summoned,' '大司马贤' is likely a title and name, '引见' means 'introduced,' '东厢' means 'eastern hall.' This phrase means 'the Grand Empress Dowager summoned the Grand Marshal Xian and introduced him to the eastern hall.'
  • 问以丧事调度 - Literal translation: 'asked about the funeral arrangements.' '问' means 'asked,' '以' means 'about,' '丧事' means 'funeral arrangements,' '调度' means 'arrangements' or 'management.' This phrase means 'asked about the funeral arrangements.'
  • 贤内忧，不能对，免冠谢 - Literal translation: 'Xian was internally worried, could not answer, and apologized by removing his hat.' '贤内忧' means 'Xian was internally worried,' '不能对' means 'could not answer,' '免冠谢' means 'removed his hat and apologized.' This phrase indicates that Xian was worried, could not answer, and apologized by removing his hat.
